model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Define the evidence, independent verification, authority, idempotency, publication, and public scope-seam contract for the issue-triage example.
source_of_truth: The deterministic fake-adapter probe, evidence-publication contract, named sixteen-contract set, frame v2, DKR-5 packet, and current public Lite and SDK validation source.
read_method: Run bash .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-5/replay.sh from the repository root and require 14 of 14 fixtures, six Standard Schema shapes, sixteen named objective contracts, eight hashes, seven wall entries, and blocked downstream advance.
questions_answered: Six Standard Schema-compatible shapes cover intake, capabilities, evidence, hypotheses, verifier verdicts, and publication receipts.
Evidence carries source, citation, capture time, expiry or maximum age, query or revision identity, and capability scope.
Repository containment, read-only SQL, bounded Victoria windows, publication authority, and idempotency keys are checked before adapters.
Publication requires a supported fully cited hypothesis and a separate verified verdict covering every citation.
Fourteen fake-adapter fixtures cover success, unsupported, missing, stale, unsafe, unauthorized, duplicate, conflicting, failed, and retry paths.
Every effect is one of six required tag ports; all logic runs through createScope and the public flow API.
The proposed sixteen names instantiate the existing objective target without changing its denominator.
questions_unanswered: The exact example package layout and adapter package ownership remain progression choices.
A separate validator has not replayed the schemas, fixtures, objective names, or active walls.
Real adapter integration remains forbidden and human-gated.
decision: candidate only: use configurable Standard Schema validation, required capability and effect tag ports, pre-effect safety gates, fresh cited evidence, separate verifier verdicts, and idempotent authority-bound publication; never accepted by this worker
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed when any schema, fixture, hash, source, freshness read, objective contract, or independent review is missing or stale

active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Count module mocks, internal reaches, global patches, and logic outside the public scope seam.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Triage flow declared dependency graph and fake call records
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm every effect comes from one of six required tag dependencies.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Safety denial fixtures and fake backend write counters
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay containment, SQL, window, authority, duplicate, conflict, and retry cases and count extra writes.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Unsupported, citation, freshness, and verifier denial fixtures
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Count publication writes after unsupported, missing, stale, failed, or writer-only verdict inputs.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Writer checkpoint without a separate validator record
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Keep the value at one until a separate validator replays every claim and wall.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: DKR-5 packet, named hashes, governed progress, and store verification
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Compare writes with the packet allowlist, resolve eight hashes, and verify the run store.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Candidate checkpoint with numeric walls and blocked independence gate
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm fixture failures stay explicit, objective denominator is unchanged, and this writer makes no acceptance claim.

wall_gate.reason: The contract is writer-only evidence; independent replay is required before CKR or PKR promotion.

confidence_probability_update: 0.4 before DKR-5; 0.91 after six schemas, fourteen fixtures, eight explicit ports, and sixteen named contracts replayed
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: All external capabilities remain fake, explicit, and scope-substitutable.
Unsafe input and missing authority stop before adapters; evidence and verdict failures stop before publication.
Idempotency reuses known receipts, rejects conflicts, and prevents duplicate writes.
The objective denominator remains sixteen; only candidate names are proposed.

candidate_pkrs.scope: Build the runnable example only after independent DKR-5 acceptance.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
